title: Number stored as Text error
author: eli8levit
body:
In my code I'm formatting the numbers to strings with commas separators this way: `num.toLocaleString('en')` in order to get something like that:
![acreen](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31045769/34827591-58daa6a8-f6e3-11e7-8d9f-2031e238cb3a.PNG)
The problem is that this green sign appears and writes me "Number stored as Text" and that I need to convert it back to number manually. I didn't find a feature that enables excel 1000 separator or ignores errors in this package. Is there something to do with it?

comments.author: magp86
comments.body:
Hi,

I have the same problem, How did you resolve it?
I put at configuration at cell 
style: { numFmt : "#,##0" }, but It doesn´t work

comments.author: Dalfons0
comments.body: Hi @JediYozh, could you please share the solution for this? Because It happens also to me and I didn't find any solution.

comments.author: junaidinam
comments.body:
Hi,
I was able to find solution for this. You have to return Number and numFmt should be #,#

comments.author: mllrdev-miguelsobong
comments.body:
> Hi,
> I was able to find solution for this. You have to return Number and numFmt should be #,#

Can you elaborate please?

comments.author: francisstefan
comments.body:
> Hi,
> I was able to find solution for this. You have to return Number and numFmt should be #,#

Hey @junaidinam, Could you please show me how to remove this warning?

comments.author: KatElizabeth
comments.body:
> > Hi,
> > I was able to find solution for this. You have to return Number and numFmt should be #,#
> 
> Can you elaborate please?

When he says you need to return number, it means the type of data you are putting into exceljs for that row or column, should be a Number and not a string. Instead of using the toLocaleString, maybe you can just write in code the format you want so that it is never a string and uses something else to place decimals etc. Like in angular, I use the number pipe:
{{yourField | number: '1.2-2'}}

When he's talking about number formats, numFmt, I am fairly sure he is referencing actual excel number formats, which is what you put into your code when exporting. You can read about excel number formats here:
https://www.excel-easy.com/examples/custom-number-format.html

For me, I had been using the method toFixed() on my data to get 2 decimal places, and it was turning it into a string. I removed that to keep it as a number, and when exported to excel, I no longer have the issue.

comments.author: macaranduba
acss1881
Aymkdn
comments.body: The format `numFmt: '$#,##0.00'` works. In my case, the issue was for the `blank` values because I used `""`. I replaced it with `null` and now the whole column is seen as a number.

comments.author: joatansampaio
